description: Page 6, 89-45-2: no record of commission to install.
Page 15, 90-36: Presbytery examined candidate for licensure on his views by a commission rather than from the floor (BCO 19-2.C, 15-2).
Page 4 "Candidate" 4th paragraph: Either the internship must be designated as one year in duration, (BCO 18-2), or as approved on previous experience (BCO 19-16) but neither is mentioned here.
Page 3, 43-10: Licentiate from another presbytery not examined according to BCO 19-5.
Page 4, 44-10.B: Education credentials/internship: approval of internship omitted (BCO 21-4).
Page 5, 44-10.C.2: No evidence of congregational concurrence in the dissolution of pastoral relationship (BCO 23-1).
Only one set of Minutes from 1989 was received for review. Standing rules require four stated meetings per year and BCO 13-11 requires Presbytery to meet at least twice per year.

Page 254, XXXVIII, Appendix 4: Commission to ordain - quorum by Presbytery listed as one TE and one RE. BCO 15-2 requires two TEs and two REs. Minutes of Commission dated 10/5/89 indicate one TE and two REs present.
Page 6: Item 3-11 of proposed BCO amendments were voted on as a unit (BCO 26-4).

Page 6, #18: in dealing with a complaint against a Session, the Presbytery allowed the ordination of Ruling Elder and a Deacon to stand, even though the RE and Deacon did not subscribe to our doctrinal standards regarding infant baptism and the extent of the atonement. (BCO 24-5).
Page 5, #28: Presbytery has set aside the requirements of BCO 24-6.
Page 11, #26, 27: Presbytery acted out of accordance with BCO 24-6 regarding, suspension of RE and Deacon who were found out of accord with doctrinal standards of PCA.
Page 7, #20: Presbytery "annuls" ordination of ordained officers in a local church - no basis in BCO.
Page 7, #20: Presbytery failed to comply with BCO 31, 32, 33 with regard to original jurisdiction in this matter.

Page 14.10: Removal of TE from roll of Presbytery - The requirements of neither BCO 38-2 nor 38-3 appear to have been met - no record of reason. The letter requested only that the pastoral relations be dissolved. no record of examination of TE. No effort to dissuade him of his views, etc. No mention he had joined another body, etc.
Page 8, #12: Licentiate preached sermon, but no record of written sermon (BCO 19-2-D).
Item 5: No record of appointment of commission to install.
Page 39, 17.3.3: No record that TE being received from another denomination was asked the questions of BCO 21-5.
Pages 68-69, 17.30.2: Moderator did not follow BCO 19-4 and 19-10 for applicant for licensure and internship.
Page 2, Item 1: Exam not specified as to content (BCO 13-6).
Page 3, Item 7: Motion to divest needs recorded 2/3 vote (BCO 34-10).
No annual reports of candidates under care (BCO 21-6).
Page 125: no record of the membership and assistance committee being appointed a Commission. Page 133 says it was the direction of presbytery, but no record of when, nor was quorum established.
Page 137: Report of Commission dated 9/25/89 is missing.
Page 4: BCO 13-9 dissolution of pastoral relationship should be carried out under guidelines of Rules of Discipline, Part II, Chapters 27-46.
Page 5: Presbytery accepted a committee's examination in lieu of an examination on the floor of Presbytery. (BCO 13-6).
Page 6(3): No listing of TE's exception to WCF.
Page 7(7)(8) and Page (10): Business acted upon that was not set forth in call (BCO 13-11).
Page 29(41)1: BCO 13-2 requires full concurrence of Presbytery within whose bounds a TE is laboring out of bounds.
Page 31(44)3.p.4: Insufficient grounds for omitting oral sermon (BCO 19-2).
Page 32(49)3: No mention made of vote of congregation to release pastor. (BCO 23-1).
15 b(2): No examination on sacraments (BCO 19-2).
16.2: Presbytery instructed CE Committee to issue a call to TE to labor in a work apparently outside of Presbytery's jurisdiction.
Page 34,35: 89-82(1): BCO 5-3 states three ways to form a "Temporary Government." Presbytery has used none of the three but a mixture of two.
Page 36, 89-83: No indication organizing commission examined prospective ruling elders according to BCO 13-8.
90-14: No approval of theological thesis and exegetical paper.
90-14: No oral examination on Bible Content.
Page 6 - Minutes do not indicate that examination for ordination (2 men) included exam on Church History.

Page 9, Improper ordination: Presbytery waived having a man come under care - BCO provides no waiver for this. Presbytery waived internship without the required 3/4 vote. No indication of theological or college degree (BCO 21-4). No assurance that work to which man was called meets requirements of BCO 8-7. No indication of theological thesis or exegetical paper required for ordination.
Page 3, #20: No record of congregational meeting to dissolve pastoral relationship to church (BCO 23-1).
Page 3, #13: Ordination process shows no record of fulfillment of BCO 21-4, par. 1, Educational requirements, only of "passing exams" (cf. par.3).
Page 1, #9: Report of commission to ordain not included (only order of service included, no date).
Page 3: No record that Presbytery approved actions of Commission as recorded in attached minutes of the Commission (dated 6/3/89) (BCO 15-1).
Page 8: No indication man was examined in Greek and Hebrew or that appropriate seminary studies were substituted.
Page 8: No indication 3 men were asked whether their views regarding the WCF had changed.
Page 8: No record of call for TE being received or reason listed for being received without a call. (BCO 13-5).
Page 5: Session minutes not reviewed or reported on within the year (BCO 12-7,40-1).
The Minutes do not record that the Session minutes of the member churches were examined during one year period (BCO 12-7, 40-1).
Page 90-6, paragraph 18-b: Exam notes exception to WCF which is not listed.
Page 6: No record of licentiate coming for ordination as having served as intern or of waiving of this requirement.
Page 13: (twice) No record of written sermon by licentiate candidates (BCO 19-2).
Page 13: TE received from another denomination not asked ordination questions (BCO 13-6).
No reports on interns in Minutes (BCO 19-12).
No record of Annual Report on candidates (BCO 18-6).
17.238: BCO 19-7 through 19-16 requires an internship. Presbytery has not mentioned its completion or equivalency for ordinand at the time of his trials for ordination.
Pages 2 & 3: No record that questions of ordination were put to candidate (BCO 13-6).
Page 4, #4: No record of reasons for waiving the Hebrew language examination and 3/4 vote required (BCO 21-4).
Page 4: No record that candidate completed internship (BCO 13-11).
Page 5, #5: The record does not indicate that candidate was examined in Christian Experience and inward call to preach (BCO 19-2-A).
Page 4, #2.4 and Attachment #10: Commission cannot add new members to itself (BCO 15-2).

p. 16 (89-64 & 66) Attachments missing (call).
response: The presbytery had responded that the call is a part of their permanent record and listed in an index of attachments, that correct procedure was followed, that it is their policy not to distribute such attachments, and that they do not agree that this Committee needs to see the item to verify that correct procedure was followed in this case. The Committee disagreed with the response on the basis that the presbytery did not supply the item cited in the exception.

description: p.7 b-2: Man called without a written call (BCO 20-1).
response: The presbytery had responded that the call was for an assistant pastor and disagreed that a written call was required. R&C maintains that BCO 20-1, paragraph 2, "A proper call must be written and ..." applies to assistant pastors as well as pastors and associate pastors.

description: May 14,1988: P. 5, #23: no record of congregational meeting to dissolve pastoral relationship to church. September 10,1988: p. 3, #11: Regarding examination of Mr. Hayes: the minutes do not reflect that BCO 19-2 was followed with regard to experiential religion and with regard to approval of a written sermon and an oral presentation of the same before Presbytery or its committee; p. 3, #16: Commission report not included in Minutes or approved; p. 3, #17: Standing rules were changed without a recorded vote; p. 4, #20: Regarding examination of Mr. Wrigley: the Minutes do not reflect that BCO 19-2 was followed with regard to experiential religion and with regard to approval of a written sermon and an oral presentation of the same before Presbytery or its committee; p. 4, #21: In examination of Mr. Wrigley the Minutes give no indication that BCO 21-4 was followed with regard to experiential religion, knowledge of the languages, PCA history, and principles of church government, nor in the sacraments; p. 4, #21: a committee may not approve a call nor may it establish a commission. November 12,1988: p. 3, #18: no record of approval of call, ordination, nor installation of Mr. Wrigley (see 9/10/88, #21). March 11,1989: p. 3, #14: in reception of Teaching Elders into Presbytery, the Minutes do not reflect that the requirements of BCO 13-6 (questions to be asked of ordinands - BCO 23-5) nor the educational requirements were met (BCO 13-6/14-4).
response: For most of the items above, the presbytery disagreed with our previous findings and did not verify that the missing or erroneous information had been corrected. For example, in exceptions regarding examinations, they felt that it was sufficient to indicate a general reference to a BCO section rather than detailing the parts. This is contrary to what was requested by the 17th GA, and this Committee concurs that we must have some verification of the details.

description: Response to 17th GA exception not approved; Central Florida cited for failing to send sufficient Minutes for review again this year.
response: 18th GA directed Central Florida to respond at their next Stated meeting.

description: Failed to submit response to exceptions taken by the 17th GA.
response: 18th GA cited Calvary Presbytery and directed them to respond at their next stated meeting.

April 25-26,1986: p.3, #16.a: No record of either theological thesis or exegesis paper being approved or omitted in trial for ordination (BCO 21-4). September 26-27,1986: p.4, #12.B.(5): waiver granted to candidate for care of two month filing requirement of his application with clerk of presbytery (BCO 18-2); p.4, #12.B.(6): same as preceding item; p.4, #12.B.(7): No record of licentiate possessing the necessary academic degrees for ordination and/or being examined in the original languages nor a record of presbytery approving or omitting required theological thesis and exegesis paper for ordination trials (BCO 21-4).
response: In the case of examinations, the presbytery felt that the use of the term "written testimonials" in the minutes covered these items. Committee disagrees in that these items should be detailed, and the presbytery should certify that the items in question have been approved. In the case of the filing requirement, the presbytery notes that they had recorded "due to extraordinary circumstances". Committee disagrees that this is sufficient - that the extraordinary circumstances should be specified.
